items.title: Final: Mercury 86, Sparks 82. Los Angeles had a shot to tie in the final 20 seconds but Dearica Hamby’s layup rolled unkindly off the rim. The Sparks have lost 5 in a row. They’re 10-16. Alyssa Thomas had 19 points, 15 assists, 11 rebounds for Phoenix. And now the All-Star break.

items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Slick Watts. Undrafted PG from Xavier LA made SEA in 73 as hustler. Led NBA w/8.1 apg & 3.2 spg in 75-76. Unique player who wore crooked headbands & shaved his dome before it was a thing. After brief peak, was out of NBA by 79 (age 27). RIP Slick.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst

items.body_excerpt: Olivia Miles has had an answer for most coverages, intrigued to see how defenses want to deal with her and Napheesa Collier in pick and roll. Storm switched, scrammed out of the matchup and that's...still a lot of space for Phee to work with.

items.title: Can't say I'm surprised about KC stepping away. It's exactly what happened in Carolina on both the radio and TV side with their legendary broadcasters. It still sucks, though. Going to miss having him around. The best guy in addition to being a legendary announcer.
items.author: Sean Highkin
items.body_excerpt: Can't say I'm surprised about KC stepping away. It's exactly what happened in Carolina on both the radio and TV side with their legendary broadcasters. It still sucks, though. Going to miss having him around. The best guy in addition to being a legendary announcer.

items.title: The Mercury lead the Sparks 19-18 after one quarter of play. Dearica Hamby scored the first seven points for LA and then went quiet offensively. She remains the Sparks’ top scorer as the team struggled to put points on the board. They went 6-23 from the field in the first.
